title: Bytes, Text I/O, Encodings, Newlines, and Cleanup · Language Clinic: Explicit Encoding
summary: A Bookbot relies on the platform default encoding, silently replaces invalid bytes, translates newlines differently by platform, reads the entire file twice, and leaks a handle on failure. Predict the user decision, input identity, text policy, expected report, failure boundary, and evidence that could disprove the proposed implementation before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the explicit encoding increment in explicit text-I/O boundary. Learners progress through…
objectives: Choose and document UTF-8 or another declared encoding instead of relying on locale-dependent defaults.
Distinguish raw bytes, decoded str values, code points, grapheme clusters, and serialized output at every boundary.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Predict Explicit Encoding · Theory Explicit Encoding

content.code:
# Worked focus: bookbot-explicit-encoding — Choose and document UTF-8 or another declared encoding instead of relying on locale-dependent defaults.
# Evidence: bookbot-worked-bookbot-text-io-encoding-3
# Competency: bookbot-worked-bookbot-text-io-encoding-3.
# Case 5798: a large-file migration rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: retain source permission, digest purpose, revision, Python, Unicode, policy, command, and output identity.
# Changed case: split one word and one UTF-8 sequence across stream chunks and compare batch output byte-for-byte.
def worked_bookbot_text_io_encoding_3(byte_count: int = 120, decoded_characters: int = 112, encoding: str = "utf-8", decode_errors: int = 0, handle_closed: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "corpusworked3",
        "policy3dekrow",
        "revision5798",
        "commandworked35798",
        "report3dekrow5798",
        "test5798worked3",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if byte_count < 0 or decoded_characters < 0:
        return False, byte_count, "io-count"
    if encoding.casefold() != "utf-8":
        return False, decoded_characters, "encoding-policy"
    if decode_errors > 0:
        return False, decode_errors, "decode-rejected"
    if not handle_closed:
        return False, byte_count, "cleanup"
    assert byte_count >= decoded_characters
    return True, decoded_characters, "decoded-text"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic pure-Python Bookbot decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected file, Unicode, stream, CLI, test, package, or release case, then state which host filesystem, terminal, subprocess, Git, install, native profile, load, security, recovery, or publication claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

# Evidence: bookbot-explicit-encoding-1ix0pc
# Competency: bookbot-explicit-encoding.

        "policycp0xi1",
        "revision7306",
        "command1ix0pc7306",
        "reportcp0xi17306",
        "test73061ix0pc",

    if byte_count < 0 or decoded_characters < 0:

    if encoding.casefold() != "utf-8":

    if decode_errors > 0:
        return False, decode_errors, "decode-rejected"
    if not handle_closed:

    assert byte_count >= decoded_characters
    return True, decoded_characters, "decoded-text"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Choose and document UTF-8 or another declared encoding instead of relying on locale-dependent defaults.

hints: For Bookbot and Python Defend the Explicit Encoding model · Theory Explicit Encoding, name the stakeholder, corpus authority, revision, path, bytes, encoding, Unicode, token, count, rank, report, command, test, package, release, and transfer boundary, Explicit Encoding precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Bookbot and Python Defend the Explicit Encoding model · Theory Explicit Encoding, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, stdout, stderr, status, test, package, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory Bookbot team handles case 6186 during a digital-humanities corpus revision. Build deterministic Python evidence to decode bytes under an explicit UTF-8 and cleanup contract without hiding malformed input; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of host file, process, Git, package-install, credential, and release effects; then split one word and one UTF-8 sequence across stream chunks and compare batch output byte-for-byte. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Choose and document UTF-8 or another declared encoding instead of relying on locale-dependent defaults.

For Bookbot and Python Defend the Explicit Encoding model · Theory Explicit Encoding, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Modern systems always use UTF-8 as the default text encoding.
